revision.text:
'''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|ja|大阪湾}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000年、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|ja|淡輪駅}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Tannowa Kofun Group|ja|淡輪古墳群}} ''' {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}} ''' {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳}} * {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}} * {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.town.misaki.osaka.jp/syogaigakusyu/sy_page/sy_page_0004.html 宇度墓古墳 - 岬町ホームページ]

'''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|ja|大阪湾}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>

Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000年、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>

Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000年、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|ja|淡輪駅}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.

revision.text:
'''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|ja|大阪湾}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000年、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|ja|淡輪駅}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.

{{reflist}}
== Related Articles ==

== External Links ==

revision.text:
'''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|ja|大阪湾}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000年、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|ja|淡輪駅}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Tannowa Kofun Group|ja|淡輪古墳群}} ''' {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}} ''' {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳}} * {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}} * {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.town.misaki.osaka.jp/syogaigakusyu/sy_page/sy_page_0004.html 宇度墓古墳 - 岬町ホームページ]

pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[sn:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[sn:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[sn:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

'''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|ja|大阪湾}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000年、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|ja|淡輪駅}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.

It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|ja|大阪湾}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000年、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|ja|淡輪駅}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.

{{reflist}}
== Related Articles ==

Together with the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000年、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|ja|淡輪駅}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.

{{reflist}}

'''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|ja|大阪湾}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>

Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Together with the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000年、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|ja|淡輪駅}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.

'''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==

The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>

Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000年、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>

'''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|ja|大阪湾}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000年、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>

Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000年、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|ja|淡輪駅}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.

revision.text:
'''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|ja|大阪湾}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000年、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|ja|淡輪駅}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Related Articles ==

== External Links ==

The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000年、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|ja|淡輪駅}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.

'''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|ja|大阪湾}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000th year 、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|ja|淡輪駅}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.

Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000th year 、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|ja|淡輪駅}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.

'''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|ja|大阪湾}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000th year 、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|ja|淡輪駅}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.

== Related Articles ==

{{draft categories| [[Category:kk]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category: 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Misaki, Osaka]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]] [[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]] [[en:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]]

'''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|12=draft}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|ja|大阪湾|12=draft}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳|12=draft}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳|12=draft}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000th year 、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|12=draft}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=draft}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線|12=draft}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|ja|淡輪駅|12=draft}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Tannowa Kofun Group|ja|淡輪古墳群|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=draft}}
== External Links ==

[[sn:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]] [[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]] [[en:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|前方後円墳|" to "|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|"
revision.text:
'''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|12=draft|12=draft}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|ja|大阪湾|12=draft|12=draft}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000th year 、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|12=draft|12=draft}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|ja|淡輪駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Tannowa Kofun Group|ja|淡輪古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=draft|12=draft}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.town.misaki.osaka.jp/syogaigakusyu/sy_page/sy_page_0004.html 宇度墓古墳 - 岬町ホームページ]

revision.comment: 37 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳 up to rev 100496810
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 淡輪ニサンザイ古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
墳丘全景（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 宇度墓古墳 |所属 = 淡輪古墳群 |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[泉南郡]][[岬町]]淡輪 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 19|緯度秒 = 49.67 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 10|経度秒 = 43.49 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長173m（推定復原約180m）
高さ13.5m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]中-後半頃 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）[[五十瓊敷入彦命]]
（伝）[[紀小弓]] |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]]・[[形象埴輪]]など |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「宇度墓」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Osaka |ラベル = 淡輪
ニサンザイ
古墳 |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西南西 }} [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun haisho-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[五十瓊敷入彦命]]宇度墓 拝所}}]] '''淡輪ニサンザイ古墳'''（たんのわニサンザイこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[泉南郡]][[岬町]][[淡輪]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。淡輪古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''宇度墓'''（うどのはか）」として第11代[[垂仁天皇]]皇子の[[五十瓊敷入彦命]]の墓に治定されている。名称は「宇度墓古墳（うどはかこふん/うどばかこふん）」とも。 == 概要 == 大阪府の最南端、[[大阪湾]]に面した台地上に築造された古墳である。800メートルほど西にある[[西陵古墳]]（国の[[史跡]]、墳丘長210メートル）、および西陵・淡輪ニサンザイの中間にあった[[西小山古墳]]（ホタテ貝型古墳、墳丘径50mに造り出し）などとともに、淡輪古墳群を形成する。名称の「ニサンザイ」は「ミササギ（[[天皇陵|陵]]）」の転訛。現在は[[宮内庁]]の治定墓として同庁の管理下にあるが、[[2014年]]度（[[平成]]26年度）には墳丘の発掘調査が実施されている2014年度の発掘調査に関する各ニュースサイト記事。 * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開　「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”]（朝日新聞、2014年12月5日記事） * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”]（産経ニュース、2014年12月5日記事） * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開　陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”]（共同通信、2014年12月5日記事（47NEWS）） 。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西南西方に向ける。墳丘は3段築成{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。墳丘長は現存で173メートルを測るが、後世に削られており、築造時点では約180メートルであったと推測される{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。外部施設として、墳丘南側・北側の両くびれ部には方形の[[造出]]が認められる{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。墳丘外表には葺石が葺かれ、加えて須恵質の[[円筒埴輪]]列、家・盾・キヌガサ・鳥などの[[埴輪]]が認められている千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000年、p.165。。埴輪の一部は淡輪独特の技法を有し、それらは「淡輪系埴輪」と称される{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。内部施設（埋葬施設）は明らかでない。墳丘周囲には周濠が2重に巡らされている（現在は1重目のみ残存）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。この周濠付近の後円部外周部分には[[陪塚]]7基が分布し、うち6基が現存し主墳同様に宮内庁の管理下にある現地説明板。。 この淡輪ニサンザイ古墳は、出土埴輪より[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]中-後半頃の西暦[[440年]]-[[460年]]頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}。5世紀中頃に限って築造された淡輪古墳群の大型古墳3基（西陵古墳・西小山古墳・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳）のうちでは、西陵古墳に次ぎ西小山古墳と同時期の築造で{{Sfn|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}、本古墳の築造をもって当地での大型古墳の築造は終焉する。これら岬町の大型古墳群では円筒埴輪に独特の技法（淡輪技法）が見られるが、同様の技法は和歌山平野の木ノ本古墳群（[[和歌山市]]木ノ本）にも見られることから、淡輪古墳群は紀伊勢力（[[紀氏]]）との強い関わりの中で成立したと考えられている{{Sfn|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1875年]]（[[明治]]8年）3月8日、玉田山を[[五十瓊敷入彦命]]宇度墓に治定{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。 * [[1879年]]（明治12年）2月7日、玉田山の治定を取り消し{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。 * [[1880年]]（明治13年）12月28日、淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を五十瓊敷入彦命宇度墓に治定{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。 * [[1981年|1981]]-[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]56-57年）、南側隣接地の発掘調査。2重周濠確認（大阪府教育委員会）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。 * [[1983年]]度（昭和58年度）、石垣改修工事・柵設置・飛地ろ号保護工事に伴う事前調査。埴輪出土（宮内庁書陵部）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。 * [[1984年]]度（昭和59年度）、石垣改修工事に伴う立会調査。埴輪出土（宮内庁書陵部）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。 * [[2012年]]度（[[平成]]24年度）、濠内堆積土除去工事に伴う立会調査。遺構・遺物の確認なし（宮内庁書陵部）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。 * [[2014年]]度（平成26年度）、墳丘の整備工事に伴う事前調査。墳丘規模推定や埴輪出土（宮内庁書陵部）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。 == 墳丘 == [[File:750302 T Nisanzai Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳の空中写真（1975年）
{{国土航空写真}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り（括弧内の数値は推定復原値）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28}}。 * 墳丘長：173メートル（約180メートル） * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：約93メートル（約102メートル） ** 高さ：約13.5メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成。 ** 幅：約111メートル（約122メートル） ** 高さ：約12.25メートル * 造出 ** 南造出 - 2段築成。 *** 東西約24メートル×南北約21メートル（東西約26.7メートル×南北約21メートル、高さ約3.5メートル） ** 北造出 - 上面は非現存。 *** 東西約20メートル×南北約11メートル 墳丘周囲には水濠が巡らされているが、この濠の波浪により墳丘の裾部が削られており、括弧内はそれを加味して復原した場合の推定値になる{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28}}。 3段築成の墳丘は、1段目・2段目に比べて3段目が著しく高いという特徴を示す{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28}}。1段目途中までは地山の削出から、その上では盛土から構成される{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28}}。地山の作出は造出でも認められることから、造出は墳丘本体と同時期の築造とされる{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28}}。この南・北造出では埋葬施設または副葬品埋納施設の存在が推定されており、特に南造出での調査では造出が壇状施設（通常は前方部墳頂で見られる）として機能した可能性が指摘されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28}}。 == 被葬者 == 淡輪ニサンザイ古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第11代[[垂仁天皇]]皇子の'''[[五十瓊敷入彦命]]'''（いにしきいりひこのみこと）にの墓に治定している宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}。この五十瓊敷入彦命の墓について『[[日本書紀]]』『[[古事記]]』に記載はないが、『[[延喜式]]』諸陵寮では[[和泉国]][[日根郡]]に所在する「宇度墓」として見え、兆域は東西3町・南北3町、守戸3烟で遠墓としている{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}。しかし中世には荒廃して所在が失われ、[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）に『泉州志』の記載に基づき玉田山に定められたが、明治13年（[[1880年]]）に淡輪ニサンザイ古墳に改められている{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}。ただし本古墳に治定された明確な根拠はない。 考古学的には、岬町の大型前方後円墳2基はいずれも当地の経済力のみで築造されたと考えにくいことから、これらの築造において紀伊勢力（[[紀氏]]）の関与が想定される{{Sfn|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}。特に『[[日本書紀]]』[[雄略天皇]]9年条に「田身輪邑（たむわのむら = 淡輪村）」に葬られたと見える、[[5世紀]]後半の将軍の[[紀小弓]]（きのおゆみ）に比定する説もある。 そのほか、『和泉志』では紀小弓の墓とする説を挙げ、『泉州志』では西陵古墳・宇度墓古墳のどちらか一方を紀小弓の墓としてもう一方を[[紀船守]]（きのふなもり）の墓とする説を挙げる{{Sfn|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=69-70}}。ただし、この紀船守は[[8世紀]]の人物（[[731年]]-[[792年]]）になる。 == 陪塚 == 古墳には後円部を取り囲むように陪塚7基があったといわれる。現在は6基が現存し、宮内庁により宇度墓の陪冢に治定されている{{Sfn|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}。 == 交通アクセス == * 鉄道：[[南海電気鉄道]]（南海電鉄）[[南海本線]] [[淡輪駅]] （南へ徒歩すぐ） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板 * 宮内庁発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp/Publication/PDF/000/kiyo067b019.pdf 書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号]}}|publisher=[[宮内庁書陵部]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016}}}} - リンクは宮内庁「書陵部所蔵資料目録・画像公開システム」。 *** 「五十瓊敷入彦命 宇度墓整備工事予定区域の事前調査」、奥田尚 「五十瓊敷入彦命 宇度墓にみられる葺石材について」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=[[上田正昭]]「五十瓊敷入彦命」|ref={{Harvid|五十瓊敷入彦命（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=石田茂輔「宇度墓」（五十瓊敷入彦命項目内）|ref={{Harvid|宇度墓（国史）}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=淡輪古墳群|title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 27 大阪府|publisher=[[角川書店]]|isbn=4040012704|ref={{Harvid|淡輪古墳群（角川）|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28-2 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=「泉南郡」|ref={{Harvid|泉南郡（平凡社）|1986}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「宇度墓古墳」|ref={{Harvid|宇度墓古墳（平凡社）|1986}}}}。 * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=岬町の歴史|publisher=岬町|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=山田 暁・岸本直文|year=2015|chapter=淡輪ニサンザイ古墳（宇度墓）の発掘調査見学報告|title=ヒストリア 第248号|publisher=大阪歴史学会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|山田 暁・岸本直文|2015}}}} == 関連項目 == * 淡輪古墳群 ** [[西陵古墳]] ** [[西小山古墳]] * [[五十瓊敷入彦命]] * [[紀小弓]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun}} * [http://www.town.misaki.osaka.jp/syogaigakusyu/sy_page/sy_page_0004.html 遺跡・旧跡 > 宇度墓古墳] - 岬町ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:たんのわにさんさいこふん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:岬町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:紀氏|墓たんのわにさんさいこふん]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]] to [[Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 淡輪ニサンザイ古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
墳丘全景（手前に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 宇度墓古墳 |所属 = 淡輪古墳群 |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[泉南郡]][[岬町]]淡輪 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 19|緯度秒 = 49.67 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 10|経度秒 = 43.49 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長173m（推定復原約180m）
高さ13.5m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]中-後半頃 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）[[五十瓊敷入彦命]]
（伝）[[紀小弓]] |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]]・[[形象埴輪]]など |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「宇度墓」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Osaka |ラベル = 淡輪
ニサンザイ
古墳 |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西南西 }} [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun haisho-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[五十瓊敷入彦命]]宇度墓 拝所}}]] '''淡輪ニサンザイ古墳'''（たんのわニサンザイこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[泉南郡]][[岬町]][[淡輪]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。淡輪古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''宇度墓'''（うどのはか）」として第11代[[垂仁天皇]]皇子の[[五十瓊敷入彦命]]の墓に治定されている。名称は「宇度墓古墳（うどはかこふん/うどばかこふん）」とも。 == 概要 == 大阪府の最南端、[[大阪湾]]に面した台地上に築造された古墳である。800メートルほど西にある[[西陵古墳]]（国の[[史跡]]、墳丘長210メートル）、および西陵・淡輪ニサンザイの中間にあった[[西小山古墳]]（ホタテ貝型古墳、墳丘径50mに造り出し）などとともに、淡輪古墳群を形成する。名称の「ニサンザイ」は「ミササギ（[[天皇陵|陵]]）」の転訛。現在は[[宮内庁]]の治定墓として同庁の管理下にあるが、[[2014年]]度（[[平成]]26年度）には墳丘の発掘調査が実施されている2014年度の発掘調査に関する各ニュースサイト記事。 * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開　「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”]（朝日新聞、2014年12月5日記事） * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”]（産経ニュース、2014年12月5日記事） * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開　陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”]（共同通信、2014年12月5日記事（47NEWS）） 。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西南西方に向ける。墳丘は3段築成{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。墳丘長は現存で173メートルを測るが、後世に削られており、築造時点では約180メートルであったと推測される{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。外部施設として、墳丘南側・北側の両くびれ部には方形の[[造出]]が認められる{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。墳丘外表には葺石が葺かれ、加えて須恵質の[[円筒埴輪]]列、家・盾・キヌガサ・鳥などの[[埴輪]]が認められている千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000年、p.165。。埴輪の一部は淡輪独特の技法を有し、それらは「淡輪系埴輪」と称される{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。内部施設（埋葬施設）は明らかでない。墳丘周囲には周濠が2重に巡らされている（現在は1重目のみ残存）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。この周濠付近の後円部外周部分には[[陪塚]]7基が分布し、うち6基が現存し主墳同様に宮内庁の管理下にある現地説明板。。 この淡輪ニサンザイ古墳は、出土埴輪より[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]中-後半頃の西暦[[440年]]-[[460年]]頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}。5世紀中頃に限って築造された淡輪古墳群の大型古墳3基（西陵古墳・西小山古墳・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳）のうちでは、西陵古墳に次ぎ西小山古墳と同時期の築造で{{Sfn|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}、本古墳の築造をもって当地での大型古墳の築造は終焉する。これら岬町の大型古墳群では円筒埴輪に独特の技法（淡輪技法）が見られるが、同様の技法は和歌山平野の木ノ本古墳群（[[和歌山市]]木ノ本）にも見られることから、淡輪古墳群は紀伊勢力（[[紀氏]]）との強い関わりの中で成立したと考えられている{{Sfn|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1875年]]（[[明治]]8年）3月8日、玉田山を[[五十瓊敷入彦命]]宇度墓に治定{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。 * [[1879年]]（明治12年）2月7日、玉田山の治定を取り消し{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。 * [[1880年]]（明治13年）12月28日、淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を五十瓊敷入彦命宇度墓に治定{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。 * [[1981年|1981]]-[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]56-57年）、南側隣接地の発掘調査。2重周濠確認（大阪府教育委員会）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。 * [[1983年]]度（昭和58年度）、石垣改修工事・柵設置・飛地ろ号保護工事に伴う事前調査。埴輪出土（宮内庁書陵部）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。 * [[1984年]]度（昭和59年度）、石垣改修工事に伴う立会調査。埴輪出土（宮内庁書陵部）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。 * [[2012年]]度（[[平成]]24年度）、濠内堆積土除去工事に伴う立会調査。遺構・遺物の確認なし（宮内庁書陵部）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。 * [[2014年]]度（平成26年度）、墳丘の整備工事に伴う事前調査。墳丘規模推定や埴輪出土（宮内庁書陵部）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}。 == 墳丘 == [[File:750302 T Nisanzai Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳の空中写真（1975年）
{{国土航空写真}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り（括弧内の数値は推定復原値）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28}}。 * 墳丘長：173メートル（約180メートル） * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：約93メートル（約102メートル） ** 高さ：約13.5メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成。 ** 幅：約111メートル（約122メートル） ** 高さ：約12.25メートル * 造出 ** 南造出 - 2段築成。 *** 東西約24メートル×南北約21メートル（東西約26.7メートル×南北約21メートル、高さ約3.5メートル） ** 北造出 - 上面は非現存。 *** 東西約20メートル×南北約11メートル 墳丘周囲には水濠が巡らされているが、この濠の波浪により墳丘の裾部が削られており、括弧内はそれを加味して復原した場合の推定値になる{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28}}。 3段築成の墳丘は、1段目・2段目に比べて3段目が著しく高いという特徴を示す{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28}}。1段目途中までは地山の削出から、その上では盛土から構成される{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28}}。地山の作出は造出でも認められることから、造出は墳丘本体と同時期の築造とされる{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28}}。この南・北造出では埋葬施設または副葬品埋納施設の存在が推定されており、特に南造出での調査では造出が壇状施設（通常は前方部墳頂で見られる）として機能した可能性が指摘されている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28}}。 == 被葬者 == 淡輪ニサンザイ古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第11代[[垂仁天皇]]皇子の'''[[五十瓊敷入彦命]]'''（いにしきいりひこのみこと）にの墓に治定している宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}。この五十瓊敷入彦命の墓について『[[日本書紀]]』『[[古事記]]』に記載はないが、『[[延喜式]]』諸陵寮では[[和泉国]][[日根郡]]に所在する「宇度墓」として見え、兆域は東西3町・南北3町、守戸3烟で遠墓としている{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}。しかし中世には荒廃して所在が失われ、[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）に『泉州志』の記載に基づき玉田山に定められたが、明治13年（[[1880年]]）に淡輪ニサンザイ古墳に改められている{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}。ただし本古墳に治定された明確な根拠はない。 考古学的には、岬町の大型前方後円墳2基はいずれも当地の経済力のみで築造されたと考えにくいことから、これらの築造において紀伊勢力（[[紀氏]]）の関与が想定される{{Sfn|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}。特に『[[日本書紀]]』[[雄略天皇]]9年条に「田身輪邑（たむわのむら = 淡輪村）」に葬られたと見える、[[5世紀]]後半の将軍の[[紀小弓]]（きのおゆみ）に比定する説もある。 そのほか、『和泉志』では紀小弓の墓とする説を挙げ、『泉州志』では西陵古墳・宇度墓古墳のどちらか一方を紀小弓の墓としてもう一方を[[紀船守]]（きのふなもり）の墓とする説を挙げる{{Sfn|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=69-70}}。ただし、この紀船守は[[8世紀]]の人物（[[731年]]-[[792年]]）になる。 == 陪塚 == 古墳には後円部を取り囲むように陪塚7基があったといわれる。現在は6基が現存し、宮内庁により宇度墓の陪冢に治定されている{{Sfn|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}。 == 交通アクセス == * 鉄道：[[南海電気鉄道]]（南海電鉄）[[南海本線]] [[淡輪駅]] （南へ徒歩すぐ） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板 * 宮内庁発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp/Publication/PDF/000/kiyo067b019.pdf 書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号]}}|publisher=[[宮内庁書陵部]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016}}}} - リンクは宮内庁「書陵部所蔵資料目録・画像公開システム」。 *** 「五十瓊敷入彦命 宇度墓整備工事予定区域の事前調査」、奥田尚 「五十瓊敷入彦命 宇度墓にみられる葺石材について」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=[[上田正昭]]「五十瓊敷入彦命」|ref={{Harvid|五十瓊敷入彦命（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=石田茂輔「宇度墓」（五十瓊敷入彦命項目内）|ref={{Harvid|宇度墓（国史）}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=淡輪古墳群|title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 27 大阪府|publisher=[[角川書店]]|isbn=4040012704|ref={{Harvid|淡輪古墳群（角川）|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28-2 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=「泉南郡」|ref={{Harvid|泉南郡（平凡社）|1986}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「宇度墓古墳」|ref={{Harvid|宇度墓古墳（平凡社）|1986}}}}。 * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=岬町の歴史|publisher=岬町|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=山田 暁・岸本直文|year=2015|chapter=淡輪ニサンザイ古墳（宇度墓）の発掘調査見学報告|title=ヒストリア 第248号|publisher=大阪歴史学会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|山田 暁・岸本直文|2015}}}} == 関連項目 == * 淡輪古墳群 ** [[西陵古墳]] ** [[西小山古墳]] * [[五十瓊敷入彦命]] * [[紀小弓]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun}} * [http://www.town.misaki.osaka.jp/syogaigakusyu/sy_page/sy_page_0004.html 遺跡・旧跡 > 宇度墓古墳] - 岬町ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:たんのわにさんさいこふん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:岬町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:紀氏|墓たんのわにさんさいこふん]]

'''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|ja|大阪湾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000th year 、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|ja|南海本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|ja|淡輪駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Tannowa Kofun Group|ja|淡輪古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ''' {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ''' {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.town.misaki.osaka.jp/syogaigakusyu/sy_page/sy_page_0004.html 宇度墓古墳 - 岬町ホームページ]

It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Itsuninomikoto|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|ja|大阪湾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000th year 、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Itsuninomikoto|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬町の歴史|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
