[[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Misaki]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Ki clan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun}} [[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11559835}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.330464|N|135.178747|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11559835]] Wikidata name: Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking [[:en:Osaka Bay|Osaka Bay]] in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Seiryō Kofun]], located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikoyama Kofun|qq=}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], and it contained both cylindrical and figurative [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000th year 、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬の Municipal History|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii clans|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬の Municipal History|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: [[:en:Nankai Main Line|Nankai Main Line]] [[:en:Tannowa Station|Tannowa Station]] (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Tannowa Kofun Group|ja|淡輪古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Seiryō Kofun]] ''' {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikoyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii clans|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.town.misaki.osaka.jp/syogaigakusyu/sy_page/sy_page_0004.html 宇度墓古墳 - 岬町ホームページ]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.330464|N|135.178747|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11559835]] Wikidata name: Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking [[:en:Osaka Bay|Osaka Bay]] in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Seiryō Kofun]], located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikoyama Kofun|qq=}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], and it contained both cylindrical and figurative [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000th year 、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬の Municipal History|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii clans|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬の Municipal History|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: [[:en:Nankai Main Line|Nankai Main Line]] [[:en:Tannowa Station|Tannowa Station]] (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Related Articles ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.330464|N|135.178747|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11559835]] Wikidata name: Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking [[:en:Osaka Bay|Osaka Bay]] in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Seiryō Kofun]], located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikoyama Kofun|qq=}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], and it contained both cylindrical and figurative [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000th year 、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬の Municipal History|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii clans|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬の Municipal History|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: [[:en:Nankai Main Line|Nankai Main Line]] [[:en:Tannowa Station|Tannowa Station]] (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.

{{reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.330464|N|135.178747|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11559835]] Wikidata name: Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking [[:en:Osaka Bay|Osaka Bay]] in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Seiryō Kofun]], located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikoyama Kofun|qq=}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], and it contained both cylindrical and figurative [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000th year 、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬の Municipal History|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii clans|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬の Municipal History|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: [[:en:Nankai Main Line|Nankai Main Line]] [[:en:Tannowa Station|Tannowa Station]] (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.

It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}. revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.330464|N|135.178747|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11559835]] Wikidata name: Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki}}, {{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|en|Osaka Bay|WD=Q999162|lt=Osaka Bay}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Sairyō Kofun|en|Sairyō Kofun|WD=Q11629965|lt=Seiryō Kofun}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikoyama Kofun|qq=}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000th year 、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬の Municipal History|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii clans|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬の Municipal History|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|WD=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|en|Tannowa Station|WD=Q7683705|lt=Tannowa Station}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Tannowa Kofun Group|ja|淡輪古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' {{ill|Sairyō Kofun|en|Sairyō Kofun|WD=Q11629965|lt=Seiryō Kofun}} ''' {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikoyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii clans|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.town.misaki.osaka.jp/syogaigakusyu/sy_page/sy_page_0004.html 宇度墓古墳 - 岬町ホームページ]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.330464|N|135.178747|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} '''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki}}, {{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|en|Osaka Bay|WD=Q999162|lt=Osaka Bay}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Sairyō Kofun|en|Sairyō Kofun|WD=Q11629965|lt=Seiryō Kofun}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikoyama Kofun|qq=}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000th year 、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬の Municipal History|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii clans|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬の Municipal History|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|WD=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|en|Tannowa Station|WD=Q7683705|lt=Tannowa Station}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Tannowa Kofun Group|ja|淡輪古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''' {{ill|Sairyō Kofun|en|Sairyō Kofun|WD=Q11629965|lt=Seiryō Kofun}} ''' {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikoyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii clans|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.town.misaki.osaka.jp/syogaigakusyu/sy_page/sy_page_0004.html 宇度墓古墳 - 岬町ホームページ]

The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii clans|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬の Municipal History|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|WD=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line|1=Nankai Main Line}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|en|Tannowa Station|WD=Q7683705|lt=Tannowa Station|1=Tannowa Station}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun}} [[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11559835}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Ki clan]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Misaki]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.330464|N|135.178747|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} '''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki|1=Misaki, Osaka}}, {{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District|1=Sennan District, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|en|Osaka Bay|WD=Q999162|lt=Osaka Bay|1=Osaka Bay}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Sairyō Kofun|en|Sairyō Kofun|WD=Q11629965|lt=Seiryō Kofun|1=Sairyō Kofun}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikoyama Kofun|qq=}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000th year 、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬の Municipal History|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii clans|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬の Municipal History|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|WD=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line|1=Nankai Main Line}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|en|Tannowa Station|WD=Q7683705|lt=Tannowa Station|1=Tannowa Station}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.

Together with the {{ill|Sairyō Kofun|en|Sairyō Kofun|WD=Q11629965|lt=Seiryō Kofun|1=Sairyō Kofun}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikoyama Kofun|qq=}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000th year 、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬の Municipal History|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii clans|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬の Municipal History|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|WD=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line|1=Nankai Main Line}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|en|Tannowa Station|WD=Q7683705|lt=Tannowa Station|1=Tannowa Station}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.330464|N|135.178747|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} '''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki|1=Misaki, Osaka}}, {{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District|1=Sennan District, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|en|Osaka Bay|WD=Q999162|lt=Osaka Bay|1=Osaka Bay}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. [[co:commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun]][[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] [[ja:淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:淡輪御陵古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.330464|N|135.178747|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} '''Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun''' (淡輪ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki|1=Misaki, Osaka}}, {{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District|1=Sennan District, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is part of the Tannowa Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus).
While the identity of the interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has designated it as the "Udo-no-Haka" (宇度墓), attributing it to {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, a prince of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}. It is also referred to as "Udohaka Kofun."
== Overview ==
Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun was constructed on a plateau overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|en|Osaka Bay|WD=Q999162|lt=Osaka Bay|1=Osaka Bay}} in the southernmost area of Osaka Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Sairyō Kofun|en|Sairyō Kofun|WD=Q11629965|lt=Seiryō Kofun|1=Sairyō Kofun}}, located about 800 meters west (a nationally designated Historic Site with a tumulus length of 210 meters), and the {{ill|Nishikoyama Kofun|ja|西小山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikoyama Kofun|qq=}}, a scallop-shaped mound, it forms the Tannowa Kofun Group. The name "Nisanzai" is derived from "misasagi" (陵), a term for imperial tombs. The Imperial Household Agency manages the site, and archaeological investigations were conducted as recently as 2014.<ref name="2014調査">2014 excavation reports: * [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGD45WJKGD4PLZU003.html “宮内庁、大阪・岬の陵墓を公開 「淡輪ニサンザイ古墳」”] (Asahi Shimbun, December 5, 2014) * [https://web.archive.org/web/20150924130935/http://www.sankei.com/life/news/141205/lif1412050018-n1.html “宮内庁が大阪・淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開”] (Sankei News, December 5, 2014) * [http://www.47news.jp/CN/201412/CN2014120501001478.html “淡輪ニサンザイ古墳を公開 陵墓、宮内庁が発掘調査”] (Kyodo News, December 5, 2014).</ref>
The tumulus features a three-tiered structure with a west-southwest orientation. Its measured length is 173 meters, though reconstructions estimate its original length at 180 meters.<ref name="書陵部紀要">{{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第67号|2016|pp=23-28, 83-92}}</ref> The mound's surface was covered with {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}, and it contained both cylindrical and figurative {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, including houses, shields, garments, and birds. Some haniwa exhibit unique techniques, categorized as "Tannowa-style Haniwa."<ref>千賀久・梅原章一『空から見た古墳』 学生社、2000th year 、p.165。</ref> Surrounding the tumulus are two moats, though only the inner one remains visible today.<ref name="説明板">On-site explanatory board.</ref>
== Burial History ==
The exact burial contents remain unknown. However, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed during the mid-5th century CE, based on the typology of its haniwa. Its construction coincides with other large tumuli in the Tannowa Kofun Group and marks the end of major tumulus building in the region.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬の Municipal History|1995|pp=49-52}}</ref>
The Imperial Household Agency identifies the mound as the resting place of {{ill|Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-ni-shiki-iri-hiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, though this attribution lacks strong archaeological evidence.<ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> Archaeological findings suggest connections to the {{ill|Kii clans|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii clans|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, influential figures in the region during the period.<ref name="岬町">{{Harvid|岬の Municipal History|1995|pp=67-68}}</ref>
== Access ==
* By rail: {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|WD=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line|1=Nankai Main Line}} {{ill|Tannowa Station|en|Tannowa Station|WD=Q7683705|lt=Tannowa Station|1=Tannowa Station}} (a short walk south). * By road: Accessible from nearby major highways.
